scope: FQ-13P4: the FQ-13P arc closer - opening variant-vs-animation-frame distinction, item-icon stability, block/item variation follow-through, and README/doc closeout. No runtime behavior change, no new art.
summary: The final increment of the visual-consolidation arc formalizes a distinction the spec insists on: the shared <id>_NN.png file convention is used for discovery, but the game consumes it in two fundamentally different ways that must never be collapsed. VARIANT categories - blocks, enemies, players - pick exactly one pool entry deterministically and hold it for the object's lifetime, because a variant is an alternate visual form. ANIMATION categories - the opening cinematic - play the pool entries in order as time frames, because an animation frame is a moment in time; prologue.gd already advances cel_frame_index = (tick*8/TICK_HZ) % n at 8fps. FQ-13P4 writes this into data/visual_assets.json as a new frame_semantics authority and teaches scripts/asset_audit.py an ANIMATION_CATEGORIES set so opening frames report as 'frames=N ANIMATION' instead of being mislabeled a pick-one variant pool or flagged as unconsumed. It also locks the item-icon stability the spec demands: item_icon is cached and items carry no variant pool, so an inventory stack can never show a different icon between refreshes - now proven by smoke for both an art-backed item and a swatch-only item. The block/item follow-through confirms the block variant mechanism stays live-but-unauthored (drop-in art, no code, no new block IDs invented for visual variation) and that deterministic block behavior is unchanged. Housekeeping: the public README suite count moves 283 -> 298 and docs/UI_ASSET_GAPS.md marks the arc complete across P0-P4. Two fq13p4_* smoke checks bring the suite 296 -> 298. Nothing in the running game changed behavior; the run documents and audit-enforces a distinction that already existed in code.
acceptance: Opening manifest distinguishes variant (pick-one) from animation (ordered frames) via frame_semantics; audit reports opening as ANIMATION (fq13p4_frame_semantics_documented)
Inventory icons never vary between refreshes - item_icon cached, no item variant pool (fq13p4_item_icon_stable)
No new block IDs for visual variation; deterministic block behavior preserved

FQ-13P arc acceptance satisfied across P0-P4 (audit + tooling, enemy/player variants consumed and stable, UI placeholders + hooks with clean fallback, no gameplay-math effect, no unnecessary world-save state)
Suite 298/298; validator and capsule doctor green; asset_audit --strict 0/0
validation: python scripts/asset_audit.py --strict -> exit 0 (0 findings, 0 data bugs)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 298/298 PASS, 2 new fq13p4_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Document the variant-vs-animation distinction as manifest authority (frame_semantics) plus an audit ANIMATION_CATEGORIES set, rather than changing runtime behavior that was already correct
Keep item icons canonical-only and cached so a stack never changes icon per refresh; lock it with smoke
Leave block variant pools as drop-in art (mechanism live, no files, no new block IDs)
Treat opening as DEFERRED animation - documented and audit-aware, not exercised until cels are authored
Close the arc in UI_ASSET_GAPS and refresh the public README suite count (283 -> 298)
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a documentation + audit-tooling increment with no runtime change
risks: No opening cel frames are authored, so the animation path is documented and audit-aware but not runtime-exercised (DEFERRED)
Block/item authored variant art is a drop-in backlog item, not a code gap
next_action: FQ-14: goal panel, tutorial prompts, and playtest checklist.
